Welcome back to The Varsity, my twice-weekly private email on
all the machinations of the sports media cinematic universe. The videos, pi=
ctures, and stories emanating from L.A. are heartbreaking. My partners Pete=
r Hamby and Dylan Byers, two adopted Angelenos, discussed the harrowing sit=
uation, and the local media response, on an impactful episode of The Powers=

Norby, of course, spent nearly 40 years at ESPN, overlapping
with Preschlack, before his well-publicized, McAfee-fueled defenestration (=
 https://puck.news/behind-norby-williamson-firing-at-espn/?_gl=3D1*as8vd5*_=
up*MQ..*_gs*MQ..&gclid=3DCjwKCAiAhP67BhAVEiwA2E_9gyjdg2yHujDLk3lsch-yp0CZbo=
MTFPdppAAL71rdHu0wDc3B2Bl3fBoCQZQQAvD_BwE&utm_campaign=3DThe+Varsity+-+SUBS=
CRIBERS+%281%2F9%2F25%29&utm_content=3DThe+Varsity+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%281%2F9%2=
F25%29&utm_medium=3Demail_action&utm_source=3Dcustomer.io&utm_term=3Df6c606=
17da42abd12a ) back in April. Norby will be replacing Michael Connelly, a f=
ormer senior vice president and executive producer who left Main Street a c=
ouple
of months ago. (Norby=E2=80=99s ESPN deal ran through the end of last year,=
 I=E2=80=99m told, emancipating him to accept this job a little more than a=
 week ago.) Officially, Preschlack has hired Norby to oversee all productio=
n and programming, including live games and studio shows, for all 16 of Mai=
n Street=E2=80=99s R.S.N.s. But many assume he=E2=80=99ll have an even more=
 expansive mandate.

=C2=A0

The R.S.N. business exists solely because of
live games, and there=E2=80=99s not much that Norby can do to change how th=
ey are produced beyond ensuring consistency among Main Street=E2=80=99s Fan=
Duel-branded R.S.N.s (A Marlins game in Florida will have the same producti=
on values as a Royals game in Kansas City, etcetera.) I suspect that we=E2=
=80=99ll really see Norby=E2=80=99s influence on studio programming, which =
is where he made his biggest mark at ESPN, as a longtime talent whisperer a=
nd the custodian of SportsCenter.=C2=A0

=C2=A0

But this won=E2=80=99t be easy, either. Not only will Norby not have access=
 to a Bristol budget, he=E2=80=99ll be working within the strictures of Dia=
mond=E2=80=99s=E2=80=94sorry, Main Street=E2=80=99s=E2=80=94post-bankruptcy=
 austerity plan. And the paradigm for success on these platforms is complex=
=2E For decades, R.S.N. executives bled=
 money building out original programmi=
ng, trying to retain viewers by expanding pre- and postgame shows, airing b=
atting practice and shootarounds,
etcetera. Even the content that was able to attract audiences, like Fox Spo=
rts Net=E2=80=99s Best Damn Sports Show Period, were simply too costly to p=
roduce and didn=E2=80=99t bring in enough money. For its part, Main Street =
already has a couple of FanDuel-branded shows in its rotation. This will be=
 a fun comeback attempt, both business and professional, to watch play out.

=E2=80=8A

From the Cheap Seats
--------------------

On CFP changes: =E2=80=9CYour interview with Rich
Clark was good, but he quickly pivoted to that pro=E2=80=93bowl game rhetor=
ic that the Bowl Industrial Complex keeps feeding fans. They try to convinc=
e fans that cities that host eight football games a year are incapable of h=
osting a football game. They also seem to think that inclement weather make=
s for bad football. Yes, ASU students weren=E2=80=99t in class for the quar=
terfinals, but I=E2=80=99ll go out on a limb and say that it would be easie=
r for a majority of them to get to campus for a playoff game than
it would be to go to Atlanta. Bowl games are now mostly neutral-site games =
in NFL stadiums.=E2=80=9D =E2=80=94A Varsity subscriber

=C2=A0

More on CFP changes: =E2=80=9CGreat to hear Rich Clark=E2=80=99s pragmatism=
 that the NFL head-to-head for CFP=E2=80=99s first round is not ideal for c=
ollege or pro football. However, I=E2=80=99d be curious if he, an Air Force=
 alum, would be willing to ask his stakeholders to explore a calendar adjus=
tment
that forces Army vs. Navy to find a new weekend for their annual matchup, s=
o the CFP first round starts a week after conference championships. This wo=
uld also force the postseason awards, including Heisman, to move, but it se=
ems like that=E2=80=99s where we are headed for the overall greater good of=
 college football.=E2=80=9D =E2=80=94A Varsity subscriber

=C2=A0

On the Fubo-Disney merger: =E2=80=9CYour view of Venu as a winner
of the Disney-Fubo deal leaves me with a lingering question: What if Venu l=
aunches, and =E2=80=98Flagship=E2=80=99 never does? If Venu has all of ESPN=
 + TNT + Fox, it is by definition wider than Flagship alone. Sports gets ca=
rved out of Max, so there isn=E2=80=99t consumer duplication or confusion.=
=E2=80=9D =E2=80=94A sports business executive

=C2=A0

/Hobbs & Shaw 2 match between Roman Reigns and The Rock.=E2=80=9D =E2=80=94=

 two years, those calls were suddenly worth $4 each. So, to once again put =
